mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading horizon.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in walk-forward validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL breaching the 250.0 index threshold signals accelerating inflation that forces market repricing of future earnings and discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of threshold-crossing instances, or signal predictive accuracy (Rank IC) falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of occurrences, or interaction effect is indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.15) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading horizon.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth coincides with negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling validation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a recessionary transition signals collapsing manufacturing demand and supply chain contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal predicts negative returns but actual 20-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered regime instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 in walk-forward tests.
